title: Technical Formulation Brief — 1% Hydrocortisone Anti-Itch Cream (OTC Drug)

sections.heading: Product Overview
sections.content:
Product: 1% Hydrocortisone Anti-Itch Cream (OTC Drug)
Formulation objective and target consumer defined in discovery session.
All specifications to be confirmed during prototype development.

sections.heading: Key Active Ingredients
sections.content:
Active ingredients confirmed during discovery session.
• Selection rationale documented
• Use levels to be finalised at prototype stage
• Supplier qualification required for all actives

sections.heading: Formulation Type & Texture
sections.content:
Recommended base and texture confirmed per discovery session.
Target pH, viscosity, and appearance to be specified at formula lock.

sections.heading: Exclusions & Constraints
sections.content:
Excluded ingredients confirmed during discovery session.
All constraints documented and communicated to formulation team.

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer

title: Ingredient Compatibility & Risk Notes — 1% Hydrocortisone Anti-Itch Cream (OTC Drug)

sections.heading: Compatibility Considerations
sections.content:
Key active ingredient interactions to be assessed during prototype phase.
Preservative compatibility study required.
pH sensitivity of actives documented.

sections.heading: Stability Risk Flags
sections.content:
• Light-sensitive actives: opaque/airless packaging recommended
• Oxidation-sensitive actives: antioxidant co-actives to be considered
• Temperature-sensitive actives: cool-down addition protocol required

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before acting on it.

title: Stability & Compatibility Testing Plan — 1% Hydrocortisone Anti-Itch Cream (OTC Drug)

sections.heading: Study Types Required
sections.content:
• Accelerated stability (40°C / 75% RH)
• Real-time stability (25°C / 60% RH)
• Freeze-thaw cycling
• Photo-stability (where applicable)

sections.heading: Testing Parameters
sections.content:
Physical: Appearance, colour, odour, pH, viscosity
Chemical: Active ingredient assay (HPLC), degradants
Microbiological: TAMC, TYMC, PET per USP/Ph. Eur.

sections.heading: Timepoints
sections.content:
T=0, T=2 weeks, T=4 weeks, T=8 weeks (within development window)
T=3, 6, 12, 24 months (real-time programme)

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before acting on it.

title: Raw Material Sourcing Requirements — 1% Hydrocortisone Anti-Itch Cream (OTC Drug)

sections.heading: Key Actives — Sourcing
sections.content:
All key actives identified in discovery session.
Supplier qualification documentation required:
• Certificate of Analysis (CoA)
• Safety Data Sheet (SDS)
• Technical Data Sheet (TDS)
• REACH compliance (if EU)
• Allergen declaration

sections.heading: Supporting Ingredients
sections.content:
Emollients, humectants, emulsifier system, preservatives, and antioxidants to be finalised at formulation stage.
All to meet applicable pharmacopoeial or cosmetic grade standards.

sections.heading: Lead Time Risk
sections.content:
Specialty actives may carry 4–8 week lead times.
Sourcing must be initiated in Week 1 of the development programme.

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before acting on it.

title: Formula Development Timeline — 1% Hydrocortisone Anti-Itch Cream (OTC Drug)

sections.heading: Development Milestones
sections.content:
Week 1–2: Brief finalisation, raw material sourcing initiation
Week 3–4: First prototype (F1)
Week 5–6: Internal evaluation, stability initiation
Week 7–8: Optimised prototype (F2), reformulation if required
Week 9: Formula lock
Week 10: Handover to regulatory and manufacturing

sections.heading: Critical Path
sections.content:
• Specialty active sourcing — highest lead time risk
• Stability data availability at formula lock decision
• Regulatory jurisdiction confirmation required by Week 2

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before acting on it.
